strengths: Large 7.35-acre site provides township-scale development potential in a growing Selangor corridor
Linked house product targeting mass-market entry price appeals to the broadest buyer demographic
Shophouse component (20%) provides commercial plinth value and natural amenities for the township
weaknesses: Leasehold 99-year tenure significantly limits resale value and financing options for end-buyers
2.5 km distance from Rawang KTM station is too far for genuine TOD positioning — lacks transit premium
40% Bumi quota is the highest in the portfolio — severely constrains open-market pricing and sales velocity
Deal has been Rejected — vendor price negotiations stalled without resolution path
opportunities: If land cost can be renegotiated down 15–20%, development margin could recover to above hurdle rate
Rawang infrastructure investment (SUKE highway completion) may improve accessibility and support long-term appreciation
Affordable housing incentives (PR1MA, RUMAWIP equivalents) could unlock government co-funding if product is repositioned
threats: Rawang submarket has persistent oversupply of landed affordable housing — absorption rate below 60%/yr is likely
Leasehold stigma in Selangor market increasingly limits buyer pool, especially post-2025 financing tightening
Opportunity cost: capital and management attention is better deployed in higher-IRR deals in the current pipeline

recommendation.headline: Reject at current terms — leasehold tenure, high Bumi quota, and weak submarket fundamentals make this deal unviable.
recommendation.rationale: The Rawang Township Phase 3 deal fails on multiple dimensions: leasehold tenure limits buyer demand, the 40% Bumi quota constrains pricing, and the 2.5 km KTM distance eliminates any transit premium. Projected IRR falls below the 15% hurdle rate even before stress-testing absorption assumptions. Management time is better allocated to the Permas Jaya and Bukit Jalil deals which offer superior risk-adjusted returns.
keyConditions: Only reconsider if vendor agrees to a ≥20% land cost reduction from current asking price
Any re-engagement must be contingent on Selangor state government confirmation of Bumi quota waiver or reduction to 20%
Commission a submarket study before any new LOI — current absorption assumptions are materially optimistic
